title: Generalizable Link Adaptation for NR Systems Based on Meta Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hengyi Lin, Xiaowen Ye, Xianxin Song, Yi Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper focuses on link adaptation (LA) for modulation-coding scheme selection in new radio (NR) systems with outdated and imperfect channel quality indicator (CQI). While existing studies have proposed deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based LA techniques for specific scenarios, these approaches lack generalizability across diverse CQI feedback delays or reporting periods and often require time-consuming retraining. To circumvent this issue, we put forth MetaLA, a generalizable LA technique based on DRL with meta-learning. By learning a meta-policy during training, MetaLA attains fast adaptation to unseen CQI feedback configurations. Besides, to enhance the learning efficiency of MetaLA, we design a new experience replay mechanism called prioritized and recent experience replay (PRER) that emphasizes high-value recent experiences during training. Simulation results show that MetaLA outperforms benchmarks in data rate, convergence speed, and generalization to unseen CQI feedback configurations. We also demonstrate the superior performance of PRER compared to conventional ones.

title: Emergent Bifurcations in Quantum Circuit Stability from Hidden Parameter Statistics
authors: Pilsung Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering
abstract: Circuit compression is a key requirement for near-term quantum computing, yet the factors that govern stability under gate removal are not fully understood. We study this problem via a large-scale numerical analysis of 300 structurally uniform circuits across 10, 12, and 14 qubits. Despite identical macroscopic resources, each ensemble separates into two stability classes under the compression settings considered here. We show that this discrete separation is not explained by structural descriptors alone, but is instead linked to the statistical properties of the gate rotation parameters. In particular, fragile circuits exhibit a consistent signature of statistical brittleness, characterized by reduced parameter variability and a lower prevalence of small-angle gates. We further identify a mechanism consistent with this behavior: paradoxical importance, where smaller-angle gates can be disproportionately critical to circuit function, an effect most pronounced in fragile circuits. These results suggest simple parameter-statistics-based diagnostics that complement macroscopic resource metrics when assessing stability under compilation-based and pruning-based compression.

title: Sustainable Recycling of Waste Aramid Paper Enabled by PAR Nanofiber Interlocking for High-Performance Insulation Applications
authors: Xuyang Yan, Jingxian Wang, Yixuan Lin, Xinyue Xia, Junjie Wang, Hanwen Zhang, Hua Wang, Siwei Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The consumption of aramid paper has increased substantially from over 2,000 tons in 2017 to nearly 7,000 tons in 2024, with projections indicating a potential doubling by 2028. However, several thousand tons of waste aramid paper are generated annually and are predominantly disposed of via landfilling or incineration, resulting in significant resource loss and environmental damage. In this study, an eco-friendly recycling strategy is proposed in which waste meta-aramid (PMIA) fibers are combined with self-prepared polyarylate nanofibers (PAR NFs) to fabricate a biomimetic, “vine-like” PAR/PMIA nanocomposite paper (PAR/PMIA NCP). The fabrication process employs a “wet disintegration/in-situ thermal welding” approach, whereby a three-dimensional interlocked preform is first constructed by wet pulping. During hot pressing, the melt processability of PAR NFs promotes interfacial π-π stacking and hydrogen bonding, forming a stable cross-linked network that enhances the structural integrity of the composite paper. The resulting PAR/PMIA NCP exhibits a breakdown strength of 129.7 kV/mm and a tensile strength of 42.4 MPa, representing a substantial improvement over commercial aramid paper. The material maintains high breakdown strength after 24 h of UV exposure and thermal aging at 200 °C and retains approximately 90% of its initial strength after repeated bending. Moreover, the paper can be recycled via mechanical fragmentation and re-compositing with PAR NFs, providing a scalable and sustainable route for repurposing waste aramid paper into high-performance insulating materials.

title: Event-Triggered Adaptive Neural Trajectory Tracking Control of Uncrewed Surface Vehicle With Output Constraints and Quantization
authors: Jun Ning, Liangtao Jin, C. L. Philip Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: This paper develops an event-triggered adaptive neural control scheme for uncrewed surface vehicle (USV) trajectory tracking control, considering input delay, time-varying output constraints, and saturated input quantization. An asymmetric Barrier Lyapunov Function (BLF) is employed within a backstepping framework to guarantee constraint satisfaction throughout the control process. To mitigate the adverse effects of input delay, the Pade approximation method and an auxiliary state variable are employed to handle the input delay effectively. The auxiliary dynamic filter is designed to compensate for the effects of input saturation. To address model uncertainties, radial basis function neural networks (RBFNNs) are utilized for accurate function approximation. In addition, an event-triggered mechanism is designed to reduce communication burden by suppressing redundant control updates. To further save communication resources, control input signals are quantized. The proposed approach is theoretically validated through Lyapunov-based stability analysis and numerically verified via simulation cases.

title: Improving 3D Gaussian Splatting Compression by Scene-Adaptive Lattice Vector Quantization
authors: Hao Xu, Xiaolin Wu, Xi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) is rapidly gaining popularity for its photorealistic rendering quality and real-time performance, but it generates massive amounts of data. Hence compressing 3DGS data is necessary for the cost effectiveness of 3DGS models. Recently, several anchor-based neural compression methods have been proposed, achieving good 3DGS compression performance. However, they all rely on uniform scalar quantization (USQ) due to its simplicity. A tantalizing question is whether more sophisticated quantizers can improve the current 3DGS compression methods with very little extra overhead and minimal change to the system. The answer is yes by replacing USQ with lattice vector quantization (LVQ). To better capture scene-specific characteristics, we optimize the lattice basis for each scene, improving LVQ’s adaptability and R-D efficiency. This scene-adaptive LVQ (SALVQ) strikes a balance between the R-D efficiency of vector quantization and the low complexity of USQ. SALVQ can be seamlessly integrated into existing 3DGS compression architectures, enhancing their R-D performance with minimal modifications and computational overhead. Moreover, by scaling the lattice basis vectors, SALVQ can dynamically adjust lattice density, enabling a single model to accommodate multiple bit rate targets. This flexibility eliminates the need to train separate models for different compression levels, significantly reducing training time and memory consumption.

title: Chat-Driven Reconfiguration of Model Predictive Control
authors: Yuya Miyaoka, Masaki Inoue, José M. Maestre
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Traditional control personalization requires users to understand optimization parameters and provide repetitive numerical feedback, creating significant barriers for nonexpert users. To deal with this issue, we propose ChatMPC, a model predictive control framework that enables users to personalize control systems and adapt to environmental changes through natural language interaction. The framework operates in two modes: personalization, where users iteratively adjust control behavior to their preferences, and codevelopment, where users provide real-time environmental information that complements sensor data. We establish convergence guarantees under different user behavior models, demonstrating exponential convergence for consistent feedback and finite-time convergence with logarithmic interaction complexity for tolerance-based users. We validate ChatMPC through simulation experiments in robot navigation with personalized obstacle avoidance and semi-autonomous driving with conversational obstacle reporting. Both experiments achieve real-time performance and demonstrate effective adaptation to user preferences and environmental changes.

title: Generative AI-enabled energy-efficient CRSNs for Consumer electronics applications in smart healthcare: An IoT Approach
authors: Amrit Mukherjee, Pavan Paikrao, Pratik Goswami, Sourav Banerjee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: The work presents a generative AI-driven framework for enhancing energy efficiency in spectrum sensing within Cognitive Radio Sensor Networks (CRSNs) for consumer electronics. Using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), the approach generates synthetic training data to compensate for limited real-world spectrum observations and dynamically adapts to changing radio environments. This reduces dependence on resource-intensive data collection while optimizing key parameters, such as sensor sleeping rates and detection thresholds, through a neural network model. This proposed work explores the integration of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) into spectrum sensing for CRSNs, by proposing a novel framework to improve energy efficiency while maintaining high detection accuracy. The work has included generative models, such as Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) and GANs, which synthesize the realistic spectrum data to train lightweight sensing models by reducing the dependency on extensive real-world data collection along with minimizing computational overhead. The study evaluates the effectiveness of the framework using mathematical modeling, which shows a significant reduction in energy consumption compared to conventional methods, without compromising the sensing reliability. In addition, the paper discusses practical implications for consumer electronics applications by highlighting scalability and adaptability to dynamic spectrum conditions and potential deployment challenges.

title: In-Context Meta-Learning-Based Model Predictive Contouring Control for Unmanned Surface Vehicles
authors: Yanming Zhou, Huiping Li, Junlong Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article proposes an in-context meta-learning-based model predictive contouring control (INCML-MPCC) framework for unmanned surface vehicles (USVs). To address the distinct dynamics across different operating speeds, we decouple the USV dynamics model into two distinct components: a shared neural network backbone and a low-dimensional context vector. Specifically, the backbone is optimized to capture shared hydrodynamic structures across diverse speed intervals, while the context vector is designed to explicitly encode task-specific residual variations. In the offline phase, restricting inner-loop adaptation to the context vector acts as an intrinsic regularizer, preventing overfitting to specific tasks. In the online phase, this adaptive model is integrated into the MPCC optimization as a predictive model. Here, the backbone remains frozen to preserve general physical knowledge while the context vector is updated via a replay buffer to dynamically capture local hydrodynamic residuals. This strategy drastically reduces the number of trainable parameters and mitigates the risk of catastrophic forgetting, ensuring robust generalization to unseen operating conditions. Simulation and hardware experiments demonstrate that INCML-MPCC achieves superior tracking performance compared with the existing benchmarks.

title: A Maximum Torque Per Loss Control Strategy for IPMSM Drive System Based on Torque Closed-Loop
authors: Kai Yao, Bochao Du, Yuchen Liu, Guangshun Fu, Qianfan Zhang, Yuan Cheng, Shumei Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: For efficiency optimization of an interior permanent magnet synchronous motor (IPMSM) drive system, an adaptive maximum torque per loss control strategy based on historical data-assisted online extremum seeking is proposed. First, a comprehensive loss model of the system is established, and the nonlinear relationship between system efficiency and current angle under constant torque conditions is analyzed. Then, an estimation method for iron loss and system efficiency based on search coils is proposed, which effectively reduces dependence on motor models and parameters. Subsequently, an extremum-seeking framework based on a proportional-integral-resonant controller is developed to achieve optimal system efficiency under torque closed-loop control. Furthermore, a historical data-assisted hybrid optimization strategy is proposed to shorten search time. This method achieves adaptive system efficiency optimization and significantly improves response performance under dynamic operating conditions. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is validated on a 21-kW IPMSM drive system.

title: Multi-band, multi-GNSS polarimetric GNSS-R observations from SCOMAG airborne campaign
authors: V. Dehaye, K. Dassas, P. Fanise, N. Baghdadi, A. Camps, F. Granouillac, R. Quintart, R. Contreres, N. Mézières, E. Amani, M. Zribi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) has demonstrated strong potential for retrieving geophysical parameters related to surface properties, as confirmed by numerous in situ, airborne and satellite experiments. In particular, the recently launched ESA HydroGNSS mission aims to exploit GNSS-R observations for the monitoring of soil moisture, inland water bodies, and vegetation parameters at the global scale. In this context, this study introduces a modified version of the French airborne GNSS-R instrument GLORI (GLObal Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry Instrument), originally proposed in 2015. While the first GLORI instrument provided polarimetric measurements limited to the GPS L1 frequency band, the modified-GLORI (M-GLORI) extends these capabilities to multiple GNSS configurations, including GPS L1 and L5 as well as Galileo E1 and E5 frequency bands, fully aligned with the frequency bands used by HydroGNSS. Following a series of laboratory qualification tests, an airborne measurement campaign was conducted in June 2024, encompassing a total of five flights over southwestern France. The campaign covered a variety of land cover types, including agricultural landscapes and forested aereas, but only data acquired over agricultural plots are used for band intercomparison. These flights were conducted in parallel to in situ ground-truth measurements over more than ten agricultural test fields. This paper presents the instrument design, calibration strategy, reflectivity statistics, and band-to-band consistency analysis of the M-GLORI instrument. Preliminary analysis of the collected data indicates that the M-GLORI instrument operates within its specified performance parameters. An analysis of the performance of each configuration (frequency band, polarization) for GPS and Galileo acquisitions is presented. An inter-comparison of measurements of all proposed bands, after normalization to one incidence angle and inter-calibration, is discussed. These results highlight the strong potential of combining GPS L1 and L5 bands and Galileo E1 and E5 bands for the accurate retrieval of land surface geophysical parameters.

title: Modeling, Analysis, and Control of Active Distribution Networks with Smart Loads
authors: Shuo Yan, Mohammad Raeispour, Lasantha Meegahapola
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: Electric spring (ES) has been introduced as a fast demand response (DR) technology that can vary the power consumption of its associated noncritical load (NCL) for bus voltage support and frequency stabilization. The combined use of ES and NCL creates a new generation of smart load (SL) technology. Furthermore, the connection of many SLs forms a smart load network (SLN), which can perform coordinated grid support functions. To further understand the usefulness of SLN, this paper provides a comprehensive investigation of the modeling and analysis of SLN in distribution grids. The detailed state-space model of the SL unit is investigated, which covers the average model of the inverter circuit, outer bus voltage loop, and inner voltage loop. Based on this state-space model, a new method of calculating the power flow of an arbitrary SLN is elaborated. The method relies on the establishment of three coefficient matrices to form a system of linear equations, which is done by simply observing the physical topology of an SLN. By solving the equations, the real-time bus voltage and SL power can be readily obtained. The integration of the state-space model of SL agents and the SLN model creates a simple and powerful tool to study the performance of the SLN in providing voltage regulation, assisting further research in the control of the active distribution network (ADN). Case studies demonstrate the usefulness of the proposed SLN model in providing voltage regulation and low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) support.

title: Interturn Short-Circuit Fault Diagnosis of A BLDC Hub Motor Via Graph Neural Networks with Multiscale Feature Distillation
authors: Zhenyu Wu, Fei Yao, Hui Wang, Yongbin Liu, Cungang Hu, Siliang Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: Brushless DC motors (BLDCMs) have become the preferred choice for hub motor drives because of their high efficiency, high power density, and excellent speed–torque characteristics. As latent faults in motors, interturn short circuits (ITSCs) cause the spatial electromagnetic distribution to exhibit differential responses, which leads to scattered electromagnetic information distribution and excessive redundant information, and poses challenges for state assessment. These issues are addressed in this study by proposing a multiscale feature distillation-based graph neural network (MSFD-GNN) architecture for noninvasive online diagnosis of ITSC. First, the correlation between leakage magnetic signals and winding states under ITSC is analyzed by establishing an equivalent magnetic circuit model and incorporating finite element analysis. Second, spatially dispersed multichannel leakage magnetic signals are transformed into information-dense electromagnetic field feature maps by integrating recursive phase space reconstruction techniques. The designed MSFD module subsequently performs adaptive refinement and enhancement of fault features, followed by cascading GNNs to learn global topological features by aggregating node neighborhood information. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively identifies the severity and location of ITSC faults, achieving a comprehensive diagnostic accuracy of 99.62% across multiple operating conditions.

title: VerTE-MT: A Multi-Task Framework with Entropy-Guided Sampling for Vertebrae Segmentation and Localisation in CT
authors: Sevde Aydogdu, Razvan Caramalau, Abir Dutta, Karen A. Eley, Joshua D. Kaggie, Danail Stoyanov, Deepak M. Kalaskar, Evangelos B. Mazomenos
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Automated analysis of spinal CT involves localising, identifying and segmenting individual vertebrae. Challenges due to similarity of vertebral structures, pathological variations and rare cases (e.g. transitional vertebrae), lead to misclassifications. Prior approaches typically address segmentation and localisation separately, with multi-stage pipelines, disregarding their intrinsic relationship. This paper introduces VerTE-MT, a novel singlestage, multi-task (MT) learning framework that concurrently performs vertebrae segmentation and centroid localisation. The proposed architecture integrates a shared volumetric encoder, a Vision Transformer bottleneck for global spatial reasoning, and dual decoders for segmentation and localisation. Entropy-guided sampling dynamically prioritises under-represented vertebrae (e.g L6) enabling efficient MT learning and enhancing performance on pathological anatomies (e.g. scoliotic). In the VerSe'20 public and hidden test set, VerTE-MT outperforms existing singlestage methods, achieving average Dice score of 84.18% and 85.45% for vertebral column segmentation, 81.03% and 75.96% in L6, while reducing segmentation boundary errors with a decrease in Hausdorff Distance (HD) of up to 4.62mm and 4.04mm, respectively. It also obtains robust localisation maintaining a mean error below 10mm across spinal regions. Zero-shot validation on cadaveric and clinical CT scoliotic datasets, with mean Dice of 83.03% and 65.17% respectively, highlights VerTE-MT's potential on unseen pathological cases.

title: Dimension-Independent Channel Estimation for RIS-Assisted Communications: From Cascaded to Separate
authors: Chao Jiang, Tianyue Zheng, Jieao Zhu, Shenheng Xu, Linglong Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Channel estimation in reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) assisted communications requires high pilot overhead due to numerous RIS elements incapable of signal processing. Recently, research on sensing RIS has provided a dimension-independent channel estimation scheme with merely three pilots. Nevertheless, it assumes that the BS-RIS channel is perfectly known to the RIS and remains invariant over a prolonged period, while inducing high hardware and power consumption. To address these issues, this paper introduces a generalized approximate message passing (GAMP) based channel estimation framework to achieve dimension-independent estimation of separate channels, without the assumption of known BS-RIS channel. Specifically, we first formulate the channel estimation problem in RIS assisted communications as a compressive phase retrieval problem. Based on the phaseless power observations, we leverage the GAMP algorithm to retrieve original sparse signals, which inherently supports the sparse-sampling sensing RIS architecture. Furthermore, by exploiting the intrinsic mapping between the sparse representations of the channels and the power observations in the angular domain, we propose a learned GAMP network to enhance the convergence stability and estimation accuracy. Finally, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach can efficiently estimate both the BS-RIS and UE-RIS channels with four pilots, while eliminating the requirement for prior knowledge of the BS-RIS channel and significantly reducing hardware and power consumption.

title: Enhanced Pilot Design for Collision-Resilient Unsourced Random Access in Massive MIMO
authors: Changwei Shi, Zhaopeng Xie, Peng Kang, Xiangming Cai, Liting Guo, Yi Fang, Pingping Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: In unsourced random access (URA) system, user pilot collisions are a key challenge when multiple users select identical pilot sequences, leading to poor activity detection and decoding performance. To address this issue, this paper develops a two-stage segmented pilot (TSSP) design for collision-resilient URA in massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) system. The proposed scheme enables a substantial enlargement of the effective pilot space without increasing the physical codebook size and detection complexity. First, we construct a probabilistic collision model by leveraging the birthday-problem analogy, which provides theoretical characterization of collision events and closely matches the simulation results. Second, we propose a segmented pilot mapping and decoding framework in which the pilot bits are divided into two interconnected parts linked by common pilot bits. This structured segmentation allows the system to resolve partial collisions and reduce missed detection in high-density access scenarios. Third, we develop a joint-covariance linear minimum mean square error (JC-LMMSE) scheme that exploits the segmented pilot structure to suppress pilot contamination and improve estimation accuracy. Monte Carlo simulations show that the proposed TSSP framework achieves up to 3.5 dB gain in collision scenarios and approximately 1 dB gain in collision-free cases over existing methods, while maintaining comparable computational complexity.

title: Cooperative Drone-Vehicle Mobile Edge Computing for Low-Altitude Inspection
authors: Weidong Qi, Weifeng Zhong, Jiawen Kang, Xumin Huang, Dong In Kim, Shengli Xie, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: In large areas, mobile edge computing (MEC) systems enabled by drones, also known as unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), can provide flexible edge computing services and facilitate low-altitude inspection. Such systems are primarily limited by the computing resources and energy of the drone, as well as their reliance on cellular network infrastructure. To overcome these limitations, this paper investigates a cooperative drone-vehicle MEC system in which a ground vehicle (GV) carries an accompanying drone (AD) and a detached drone (DD) to visit multiple data collection nodes for low-altitude inspection. We develop a joint drone-vehicle model with path planning, data collection, and processing. The AD is carried by the GV to multiple nodes and collects data when the GV is stationed at a node. The DD can detach from the GV to visit other nodes and perform data collection independently. The GV and drones cooperate in data processing and energy replenishment. We propose a heuristic to minimize the low-altitude inspection mission completion time by jointly optimizing the route and the DD speeds. We analyze the relationship between the DD speed and the size of DD-processed data and design a method that includes flight power approximation to optimize the DD speed. Numerical results indicate that solutions optimized by the heuristic fully utilize the DD, thereby reducing the completion time of low-altitude inspection missions.

title: Finite-Time Stability of Switched Nonlinear Systems With Partially Unstable Modes via Hybrid Switching
authors: Jie Wu, Rongni Yang, Yanzheng Zhu, Xudong Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This article investigates the finite-time stability (FTS) problem of switched nonlinear systems (SNSs) involving partially unstable modes under hybrid switching signals. In spite of significant challenge caused by unstable modes in SNSs, the qualitative analysis of FTS can still be conducted through the comprehensive utilization of switching information. To be specific, by constructing the discontinuous multiple Lyapunov function (DMLF) coupled with persistent dwell time (PDT)-based hybrid switching, a novel feasible solution for the FTS of SNSs is established. The “discontinuous” property of DMLF implies that certain state attenuation can be artificially designed at the switching instant to compensate for system divergence, and the proposed PDT-based switching employs respectively the τ -portion and the T -portion between stable and unstable subsystems to achieve FTS. Then, the FTS criteria are proposed in terms of stabilizing and destabilizing states during the T -portion, and the hybrid switching design scheme is established for stabilizing or destabilizing state during the T -portion. In particular, the overall settling-time upper bounds depending on both the initial state and the specific dwell time are further derived. Finally, two numerical examples including an application to the finite-time synchronization of switched complex dynamical networks are presented to demonstrate the practicality and effectiveness of the proposed theoretical results.

title: Investigating the Properties of Kilohertz Coaxial Self-Resonant Structures for Inductive Power Transfer
authors: Artur M. Benedito Nunes, Yusaku Takagi, Richard A. McMahon, Hiroshi Fujimoto
publish: IEEE Journal on Wireless Power Technologies
abstract: Coaxial self-resonant systems are gaining traction as a simple yet tunable option in the design of self-resonant coils, whilst providing the typical benefits of removing the need for compensation networks and offering safer operation due to a lower voltage at the coil terminals. The authors have sourced coaxial cable, which is the simplest and cheapest cable from which a coaxial self-resonant coil can be made. It is shown in this publication that off-the-shelf coaxial cable can be used for wireless charging of electric vehicles up to 3.4 kW, and that more optimised cable/coil design may enable even higher power operation. Coil-to-coil efficiency when delivering power to a constant voltage load is around 85% but this comes with the benefit of reduced onboard space and greatly reduced coil terminal voltage. This paper is the first to thoroughly examine what is happening within the cable as it is operated in its two self-resonant modes: open mode (series-resonant) and shorted mode (parallel-resonant). The relevant aspects of coil design that one should bear in mind when constructing a kilohertz-resonant coaxial cable coil are covered and power transfer equations are derived. Coils are constructed that resonate at 260 kHz but these can be stacked in pairs to resonate at 92 kHz in a system. This frequency is achieved without the use of a ferrite core, which would further lower the operating frequency to be within the 79- 90 kHz range specified by the SAE standard for electric vehicle charging.

title: High-Quality Entity Segmentation and Grounding
authors: Lu Qi, Yi-Wen Chen, Tao Zhang, Xiangtai Li, Xu Yang, Bo Du, Ming-Hsuan Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Class-agnostic segmentation is of great importance for generalizing the localization of semantically-consistent regions from limited training data to unseen domains and categories. It has benefited numerous applications, such as human-interaction-assisted image editing. Despite their success, existing class-agnostic methods (e.g., SAM2 and entity segmentation) still struggle with boundary-accurate masks and semantic understanding. To address these issues, we propose ESG, a pipeline for high-quality entity segmentation and grounding supported by a new dataset EntitySeg. At first, the proposed dataset naming EntitySeg contains images spanning various image domains and entities, along with plentiful high-resolution images and high-quality mask annotations for training and testing. Then, the ESG mainly consists of two modules: CropFormer for high-quality entity segmentation whereas GELLA for accurate noun extraction from sentences and semantic matching between language and visual regions. Unlike existing grounding methods that jointly train a segmentation and a large language model, ESG adopts a two-stage decoupled design, preserving high-quality masks and grounding robustness without the trade-offs often introduced by joint training. CropFormer ensures high-quality entity segmentation results, which can then be encoded into the GELLA model for effective grounding. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed pipeline across five tasks, including entity segmentation, panoptic segmentation, open-vocabulary segmentation, referring segmentation, and panoptic localized narratives. Furthermore, GELLA module of ESG pipeline is highly flexible and capable of processing mask inputs from any segmentation framework, thanks to its lightweight colormap/vision encoder, language/mask decoder, and association module. The entity segmentation dataset and grounding code will be released at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/qqlu/Entity</uri>.

title: UWarp: A Whole Slide Image Registration Pipeline to Characterize Scanner-Induced Local Domain Shift
authors: Antoine Schieb, Bilal Hadjadji, Natalia Fernanda Valderrama, Daniel Tshokola Mweze, Valentin Derangère, Laurent Arnould, Sylvain Ladoire, Alain Lalande, Alessio Fiorin, Carlos López Pablo, Noèlia Gallardo Borràs, Shrief Abdelazeez, Vincenzo Della Mea, Anna Korzynska, Nathan Vinçon, Louis-Oscar Morel
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Histopathology slide digitization introduces scanner-induced domain shift that can significantly impact computational pathology models based on deep learning methods. In the state-of-the-art, this shift is often characterized at a broad scale (slide-level or dataset-level), but not at the patch level, which limits our comprehension of the impact of localized tissue characteristics on model accuracy. To address this challenge, we present a domain shift analysis framework based on UWarp, a novel registration tool designed to accurately align histological slides scanned under varying conditions. UWarp employs a hierarchical registration approach, combining global affine transformations with fine-grained local corrections to achieve robust tissue patch alignment. We evaluate UWarp using two private datasets, CypathLung and one from the BosomShield project, containing whole slide images scanned by multiple devices. Our experiments show that UWarp outperforms existing open-source registration methods, achieving a median target registration error (TRE) below 4 pixels (<1 μm at 40× magnification), compared to 21 pixels (~5 μm at 40×) for the best state-of-the-art algorithm, while significantly reducing computational time. Additionally, we perform a localized characterization of scanner-induced domain shift by analyzing the predictions of a deep learning model for breast cancer pathological response. Accurate patch-level alignment between scanners is ensured using UWarp. Our analysis reveals that prediction variability correlates with tissue versus background ratio and nuclei density at patch level, highlighting the importance of accounting for localized domain shift to improve model robustness and adaptation strategies in computational pathology.

title: EvTurb: Event Camera Guided Turbulence Removal_supp1-3697623.pdf
authors: Boxin Shi
publish: N/A
abstract: Atmospheric turbulence degrades image quality by introducing spatially varying blur and geometric tilt distortions, posing significant challenges to downstream computer vision tasks including segmentation and recognition. Existing single-image and multi-frame methods struggle with the highly ill-posed nature of this problem due to the compositional complexity of turbulence-induced distortions. To address this, we propose EvTurb, an event guided turbulence removal framework that leverages high-speed event streams to decouple blur and tilt effects. EvTurb uses event-derived cues in a novel two-step event-guided network: event integrals are first employed as empirical guidance to reduce blur in the coarse outputs. This is followed by employing a variance map, derived from raw event streams, to eliminate the tilt distortion for the refined outputs. Additionally, we present TurbEvent, the first real-captured dataset featuring diverse turbulence scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that EvTurb surpasses state-of-the-art methods while maintaining computational efficiency.

title: Optimal Temperature-Constrained Charging of IMC Trolleybuses at Low Speeds and Standstill
authors: Jakub Ševčík, Pavel Drábek, Jan Přikryl, Martin Zavřel, Zdeněk Peroutka
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: In-motion-charging (IMC) trolleybuses are battery-equipped trolleybuses whose onboard batteries are recharged from the overhead contact line (OCL) on electrified sections of the route, enabling reliable off-wire operation on the rest of the route. To avoid OCL overheating during standstill, existing charging strategies typically rely on constant or piecewise-constant current profiles derived from steady-state thermal limits; these profiles are fixed a priori and cannot exploit the transient thermal headroom available during short and unpredictable stops. This paper addresses this gap by proposing a temperature-constrained charging strategy that maximizes the energy transferred during standstill and low-speed operation without exceeding the OCL thermal limits, even when the stop duration is unknown a priori. A comprehensive experimental study was conducted to characterize the OCL’s thermal behavior and to identify a data-driven state-space model predicting the contact temperature at the wire–shoe interface. Building on this model, an optimization framework and a stepwise algorithm are proposed for direct implementation in trolleybus operation. A case study on Pilsen trolleybus lines demonstrates substantially higher charging efficiency and energy throughput than conventional constant-current control, while ensuring full compliance with the EN 50119 standard.

title: DS-LABRNav: Land-Air Bimodal Robot Navigation With Traversable Obstacles Base on Vision-Language Model
authors: Yongjie Li, Wenshuai Yu, Molong Duan, Bo Zhang, Zhou Liu, Qingquan Li
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Land–air bimodal robots (LABR) provide efficient ground locomotion and flexible aerial mobility, making them suitable for complex navigation tasks. Traditional methods cannot distinguish between traversable obstacles (such as curtains) and rigid obstacles, resulting in unnecessary aerial transitions. To address these challenges, DS-LABRNav is proposed as a navigation framework that integrates a Vision-Language Model (Align-DS-V) into land–air planning. This framework consists of an event-triggered Obstacle-Induced Takeoff Trigger (OITT) and a modality-guided occupancy map update. OITT selectively activates the VLM reasoning only prior to a proposed aerial maneuver while the robot is safely on the ground. The occupancy map update then incorporates semantic reasoning results, enabling the planner to re-optimize the trajectory and prioritize land modalities for improved energy efficiency. Specific experiments demonstrate that DS-LABRNav improves navigation efficiency and path optimality while reducing mode-switching costs. Compared to traditional map-based approaches, the framework achieves energy savings of up to 70% in scenarios with traversable obstacles and unknown slopes, validating its effectiveness in real-world LABR deployments.

title: Nezha-FX: A Morphing HAUV Designed Based on Balanced Cross-Domain Performance Optimization_supp5-3691061.mp4
authors: Hexiong Zhou
publish: N/A
abstract: As emerging multirotor-type hybrid aerial underwater vehicles capable of both flight and underwater operations, numerous conceptual prototypes have been proposed in recent years. However, existing designs lack a balanced consideration of cross-medium stability, underwater maneuverability, and mode-switching reliability within a single configuration, and there remains no unified evaluation criterion for cross-domain performance. To compensate for the limitations of conventional optimization-based design, this work leverages key constraints identified through experiments and employ a multiobjective optimization approach to achieve a balanced design among these competing requirements. A lightweight and reliable foldable-arm system is further developed to support the implementation of the optimized configuration. In addition, through systematic integration of aerial propulsion bench test data with vehicle design parameters, we develop novel static quantitative metrics (cross-domain thrust margin@50%) to comprehensively assess overall design rationality. Finally, simulation and field tests show that the underwater damping of the final prototype has decreased by 55.2%. Compared with the previous generation, the cross-domain average power consumption is reduced by 68.9%.

title: A Stiffness-Adaptive Visuo-Tactile Robotic Palpation System for Surface Texture Profiling and Deep Lesion Localization_supp2-3691370.pdf
authors: Dongliang Fan
publish: N/A
abstract: Accurate intraoperative tissue characterization relies on the ability to resolve both surface microtextures and deep-seated structures for comprehensive diagnosis. However, achieving surface profiling and deep-tissue inspection remains a critical challenge due to the difficulty of combining variable stiffness and high-fidelity perception within a compact device. Here, we propose a robotic palpation system consisting of a novel visuo-tactile sensing probe (diameter: 10 mm) integrated into a robotic platform for accurate tissue characterization. This probe exhibits adaptive strain-stiffening behavior (achieving a 470-fold stiffness variation), applicable for various surgical conditions. With our 3-D reconstruction and scanning framework, the palpation system achieves high-precision 3-D reconstruction (MAE: 28 μm in the z-axis, 3.1 μm in the xy-plane), profiling surface textures (feature size: 150 μm), and delineating the boundaries of complex deep-seated inclusions (sensitivity $ > $ 0.92). Finally, an ex vivo porcine liver model was prepared to demonstrate our system’s capability to analyze and decouple microscale superficial irregularities from macroscale lobulated deep-seated lesions under dynamic disturbances. These results confirm the system’s potential as a precise, quantitative platform for robotic surgical palpation in complex pathological scenarios.

title: An Efficient Optimal Trajectory Planning and Control Framework for Quadruped Robot Jumping Through Constrained Obstacles
authors: Rui Huang, Xinqi Li, Tianyang Tang, Jinghan Lin, Hong Lin, Peng Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Addressing the challenge of quadrupedal terrain traversal in complex environments with constrained obstacles, this work presents an integrated planning-control framework synergizing trajectory optimization (TO), nonlinear model predictive control (NMPC), and whole-body control (WBC). The core theoretical contribution of this work is to propose an enhanced multiple-shooting (MS) based constrained differential dynamic programming (DDP) solver for kinodynamic NMPC. A novel derivation is proposed that accounts for the defect between shooting segments during the DDP backward pass, which improves traditional single-shooting (SS) based DDP robustness to initial guesses. Furthermore, the NMPC is integrated with high-level TO and low-level WBC to form a unified optimal trajectory planning and control framework, enabling quadruped robots to execute high-speed dynamic motions with extended flight phases through constrained obstacles, which constitutes the main technical contribution of this research. Experimental validation via high-fidelity simulations and hardware implementation on our self-developed platform ArcDog confirms both the solver’s superiority and the efficacy of the framework.

title: RMAS: Risk Mitigation Agent System, An Autonomous Framework for Real-Time Supply Chain Risk Management
authors: Mohammad Abdul Mateen Fazal, Fawwaz Ali, Babu Kaji Baniya
publish: IEEE Engineering Management Review
abstract: Modern global supply chains face increasing volatility due to demand fluctuations, logistics disruptions, and geopolitical instability. Traditional risk management approaches remain highly reactive, relying on fragmented analytical pipelines and delayed human intervention. This paper presents the Risk Mitigation Agent System (RMAS), an autonomous event-driven architecture for real-time risk detection, analysis, and mitigation in supply chain operations. RMAS employs a multi-agent cognitive framework consisting of five layers: Sense, Diagnose, Plan, Simulate, and Act (SDPSA), collectively forming the RMAS cognitive cycle. Apache Kafka and Avro-based messaging enable high-throughput event streaming with strict schema validation. The Sense layer ingests demand, forecasting, and inventory events, while the Diagnose layer applies Large Language Models (LLMs) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for context-aware reasoning. The Plan layer uses OptaPlanner optimization to generate mitigation strategies, the Simulate layer evaluates candidate actions through discrete-event simulation, and the Act layer integrates approved actions into enterprise systems via secure API gateways. RMAS incorporates a governance-centric AUTO/HUMAN/BLOCK control mechanism that balances autonomous execution with managerial oversight for high-impact decisions. The system was evaluated using a three-month retail supply chain disruption simulation involving five distribution centers, fifty retail stores, and 10,000 Stock Keeping Units (SKUs). Experimental results show that RMAS reduces end-to-end mitigation cycle time by 85.2% compared to rule-based automation and by over 99% relative to manual workflows, lowers operating costs by 12.4%, improves service levels from 81.5% to 92.8%, increases fill rates from 89.1% to 94.7%, and maintains under 2-second detection latency at 10,000 risk events per minute.

title: Ultra-Compact Widely Tunable Dual-Passband Microwave Photonic Bandpass Filter
authors: Chongjia Huang, Lei Shan, Mengyao Wang, Rui Ma, Erwin H. W. Chan, Xinlun Cai, Ya Su, Peng Hao, X. Steve Yao
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: A microwave photonic bandpass filter capable of generating two independent frequency-tunable passbands is presented. It is based on two different-frequency laser sources followed by a photonic integrated chip consisting of a phase modulator and a microring resonator (MRR) with a pair of electrodes. The MRR resonances introduce amplitude imbalances between the two opposite-phase RF modulation sidebands. This enables RF signals at two different frequencies, corresponding to the differences between the laser source continuous wave light frequencies and the MRR resonance frequencies, to pass through the system. This is equivalent to having two passbands in the system amplitude response. The proposed dual-passband bandpass filter overcomes the drawbacks of bulky implementation, high system cost and complexity, frequency-dependent response shape, as well as noise and dynamic range issues encountered in previously reported structures. High-speed tuning of the two passband frequencies is achieved by electrically tuning the MRR resonances, while independent tuning is enabled by additionally adjusting a laser frequency. Experimental results are presented that demonstrate independent tuning of two passbands over a frequency range of 2 to 25 GHz via controlling the voltage of the driving signal applied to the MRR electrodes and adjusting the frequency of a laser source. Results also demonstrate that the 3 dB passband width remains within 385 ± 25 MHz, and the passband amplitude varies by less than ±1.5 dB during frequency tuning.

title: Without Paired Labeled Data: End-to-End Self-Supervised Learning for Drone-View Geo-Localization
authors: Zhongwei Chen, Zhao-Xu Yang, Hai-Jun Rong, Guoqi Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Drone-view geo-localization (DVGL) aims to achieve accurate localization of drones by retrieving the most relevant GNSS-tagged satellite images. However, most existing methods heavily rely on strictly prepaired drone-satellite images for supervised learning. When the target region shifts, new paired samples are typically required to adapt to the distribution changes. The high cost of annotation and the limited transferability of these methods significantly hinder the practical deployment of DVGL in open-world scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose a novel end-to-end self-supervised learning method with a shallow backbone network, called the dynamic memory-driven and neighborhood information learning (DMNIL) method. It employs a clustering algorithm to generate pseudolabels and adopts a dual-path contrastive learning framework to learn discriminative intraview representations. Furthermore, DMNIL incorporates two core modules, including the dynamic hierarchical memory learning (DHML) module andtheinformation consistency evolution learning (ICEL) module. The DHML module combines short-term and long-term memory to enhance intraview feature consistency and discriminability. Meanwhile, the ICEL module utilizes a neighborhood-driven collaborative constraint mechanism to systematically capture implicit crossview semantic correlations, consequently improving crossview feature alignment. To further stabilize and strengthen the self-supervised training process, a pseudolabel enhancement (PLE) strategy is introduced to enhance the quality of pseudo supervision. Extensive experiments on three public benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms existing self-supervised methods and even surpasses several state-of-the-art supervised methods. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ISChenawei/DMNIL</uri>

title: A Novel Online Insulation Diagnosis Scheme for HVDC Cables Based on Resonant Point Trajectory
authors: Ziyu Wei, Yang Wu, Qiuqiong Lin, Pinjia Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: High-voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission has become a key solution for integrating large-scale offshore wind power over long distances. Given that HVDC cables are the primary energy carriers in such systems, their insulation conditions are critical to overall system reliability, highlighting the urgent need for effective online insulation diagnosis. However, existing methods often encounter significant limitations in HVDC applications, such as severe signal attenuation. To address these issues, a novel online insulation diagnosis scheme for HVDC cables based on resonant point trajectory is proposed in this article. The terminal admittance of the cable connected to a modular multilevel converter (MMC) is theoretically modeled, and the characteristics of resonant point trajectory under cable aging conditions are thoroughly analyzed. Then, an online diagnosis framework is developed, comprising a resonant point extraction technique via perturbation injection in the control loop and a two-step optimization algorithm for cable aging estimation. Extensive simulations and experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme, showing an average error of less than 7% in aging severity estimation. Compared to conventional methods, the proposed approach requires no additional injection equipment and provides quantitative results for cable aging assessment.

title: Quantification-Enabled Failure Mode Separation for IGBT Module Package Degradation Assessment
authors: Xinming Yu, Jie Kong, Dazhi Wang, Dao Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Insulated-gate bipolar transistors (IGBTs) are critical elements in power electronic system, and their health directly affects system reliability. Accurate degradation assessment is hindered by the strong coupling between bond-wire and solder layer degradation. This paper proposes a reliability-oriented quantitative degradation assessment method based on wear-out failure mode separation. It decouples bond-wire and solder layer effects using two diagnostic features: the on-state voltage drop at the intersection current of the IGBT I–V curve, and the on-state voltage drop at the peak current. The proposed method can independently quantify bond-wire and solder layer faults, enabling fault separation and assessment of degradation level. The relationship between the intersection current point in the hysteresis curve and the thermal response was investigated. The case study H-bridge converter demonstrates that theoretical and experimental wear-out degradation level errors of bond-wire and solder layer do not exceed 4.6% and 5.0%, respectively. The proposed method provides a quantitative wear-out degradation estimation tool for high-reliability applications, supporting online condition assessment and optimized maintenance decisions.

title: Photovoltaic Scenario Generation via Multimodal Fusion and Local Window Attention
authors: Zhipeng Kang, Jianbo Shang, Rui Zhu, Xiaojing Zhu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The large-scale integration of photovoltaic (PV) power intensifies the uncertainty of electrical grids. Abrupt changes in PV output during diurnal transitions present challenges for high-resolution scenario generation due to deficiencies in multimodal fusion, constraints in long-sequence modelling, and inefficiencies in feature decoupling. This paper proposes a multimodal local window generative adversarial network (M-LGAN) to address these bottlenecks. M-LGAN utilizes PV-specific local window attention to capture critical-period fluctuations while reducing computational complexity. A dynamic feature balancing module integrates spatio-temporal-noise modalities to enhance spatial heterogeneity modeling. Furthermore, adversarial training with multi-scale constraints and composite loss suppresses mode collapse, preserving site-specificity. The performance of M-LGAN was evaluated on 77 PV sites at 15-minute resolution, with the results indicating a reduction in Maximum Mean Discrepancy of 9.91%-13.04% and an enhancement in Continuous Ranked Probability Score of 3.78%-7.29% in comparison to established benchmarks. Furthermore, it attains a Spatial Consistency Score of 0.0261, successfully mitigates rare-event reproduction errors by 38.5%, and enhances training efficiency by a factor of 3.2. Generated scenarios have been shown to accurately replicate gradient dynamics, thereby providing robust support for day-ahead scheduling and risk assessment in high-renewable systems.

title: Cooperative Target Fencing Safety Control for Multiple Autonomous Aerial Vehicles via Nonlinear Model Predictive Control
authors: Tinghan Jia, Huaicheng Yan, Kai Rao, Yunkai Lv, Youmin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This article investigates the fencing-tracking control problem for multiple quadrotor autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) pursuing a moving target AAV in the presence of time delays, external disturbances and external obstacles. To achieve the fencing control for the target AAV with unknown velocity, an attractive-repulsive mechanism is designed based on the relative distances between the target and neighboring AAVs, confining the target within a rigid convex hull. The rigid formation synchronizes with the velocity of the target through the velocity observers, keeping the target enclosed. Subsequently, a nonlinear Lyapunov model predictive control based on the control barrier function (NLMPC-CBF) is developed to predict the running trajectory and maintain optimal safety-critical fencing-tracking control in the presence of obstacles. To guarantee the feasibility and stability of the NLMPC-CBF, the Lyapunov-based adaptive auxiliary controller is designed and its stability is exploited to construct stability constraints. The NLMPC-CBF inherits features of the Lyapunov-based adaptive auxiliary controller and utilizes online optimization to improve cooperative control performance. The simulation results demonstrate a series of comparative experiments showing that the proposed NLMPC-CBF method significantly enhances the fencing-tracking control performance and enables the obstacle avoidance control.

title: Research on a Machine Learning-Based Method for Identifying the Distribution of Radioactive Source Terms on the Inner and Outer Walls of Metallic Pipelines
authors: Fuquan Chen, Chuanhao Hu, Guoqiang Zeng, Chundi Fan, Yongkang Deng, Na Li, Honglin Zhao, Jian Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Source term measurement of radioactive metallic pipelines remains a significant challenge in nuclear radiation measurement. As metallic pipelines age, defects such as damage and corrosion can lead to the leakage of radioactive materials onto the outer pipe wall, thereby increasing safety risks. Investigating the distribution of radioactive contaminants on both the inner and outer surfaces of metallic pipelines is therefore crucial for enhancing the safety of nuclear energy applications. In this study, a novel machine learning (ML)–based approach is proposed to identify the distribution of radioactive contamination on the inner and outer walls of metallic pipelines. Energy spectrum data acquired by a LaBr₃(Ce) detector are used to train machine learning models for classification of source distributions. The Support Vector Machine (SVM), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), and Transformer models were selected as the primary algorithms. The optimal parameters were determined using the grid search, and classification and performance comparison were conducted on energy spectrum data from different scenarios. The results show that the Transformer model outperformed the others, achieving an accuracy of 96.19%, higher than SVM (74.56%) and CNN (75.44%). This approach provides a new solution for identifying the distribution and ensuring safety of radioactive contaminants in metallic pipelines within nuclear radiation measurement scenarios.

title: A Novel Consequent-Pole Variable Flux Machine with Flux Barrier Design
authors: Ya Li, Xinhao Wang, Runyu Wang, Yunlu Du, Shichuan Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Conventional hybrid permanent-magnet (PM) variable flux machines (VFMs) typically employ a large amount of high coercive force (HCF) PMs, leading to high cost and serious magnetic saturation. Moreover, their design is often constrained by two conflicting requirements. On the one hand, extending the flux-regulation (FR) range tends to compromise on-load flux stabilization of the low coercive force (LCF) PMs. On the other hand, pursuing high torque density usually makes it difficult to maintain a wide high-efficiency operating region. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel consequent-pole VFM (CP-VFM) topology with flux barrier design to better reconcile these two inherent contradictions. The proposed design substantially reduces the usage of HCF PMs to alleviate saturation and lower material cost. By integrating the complementary merits of series and parallel magnetic circuits, the proposed CP-VFM improves the FR capability while maintaining a stable operating point of the LCF PMs. First, the topology and operating principle of the proposed machine are presented. Subsequently, an equivalent magnetic-circuit model is established and analyzed to elucidate the mechanism by which the proposed machine achieves an extended FR range. Finally, comprehensive finite-element (FE) investigations are conducted to evaluate its electromagnetic performances. The simulated results demonstrate that the proposed topology achieves a wide FR range and high torque density with a simplified structure.

title: A Spectral-spatial Adaptive Weighted Method for Shadow Removal in Vegetation Canopy Using Multi-scale UAV-based Hyperspectral Imagery
authors: Wenxuan Zhang, Kangning Li, Jinbao Jiang, Ming Wang, Di Pan, Xiaojun Qiao, Xuepeng Xiao, Xiaotong Ban
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: While unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) provide high spectral-spatial resolution and operational flexibility for hyperspectral vegetation monitoring, their high-resolution imagery remains susceptible to shadow interference, which obscures true canopy reflectance, thereby compromising classification and biophysical parameter retrieval accuracy. However, current shadow removal methods exhibit limited dynamic adaptability to varying shadow characteristics and demonstrate inconsistent performance across different spatial scales. To examine resolution effects, shadow characteristics were analyzed before shadow removal: with decreasing resolution, the absolute spectral difference between sunlit and shadowed regions diminished, the shadow area decreased, and brightness variation along shadow boundaries increased. Based on these observations, this study proposes a spectral-spatial adaptive weighted method (SAWM) for shadow removal for multi-scale UAV-based hyperspectral imagery, which consists of three steps:(1) sunlit regions were preliminarily detected using two vegetation indices and the threshold-based approach. (2) Endmembers were extracted from the sunlit regions using unsupervised algorithms and merged through K-means clustering. (3) A joint optimization framework for shadow removal was constructed by integrating the shadow multilinear mixing (SMLM) model with total variation (TV) spatial regularization, with the regularization weight adaptively determined based on the two-indicator balance points. The results show that SAWM achieved a mean RMSE of 0.0768 and rRMSE of 44.09% across multiple spatial resolutions. Compared to three representative methods, SAWM achieved improvements of up to 0.0332 in RMSE and 15.94% in rRMSE. SAWM offers an adaptive solution for shadow removal in high-resolution multi-scale UAV imagery, with potential to improve vegetation analysis in complex environmental conditions.

title: Large-Signal Stabilization of On-Board Hybrid Power Supply Systems Supplying Constant Power Load Based on Composite Control
authors: Wenjie Ao, Jiawei Chen, Pengwei Chen, Jie Chen, Fei Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: To meet the cross-timescale response requirements of new electrified loads, lithium batteries and supercapacitors are being integrated into onboard electrical power systems via DC/DC converters. Despite the rapid response and energy recovery capabilities of these storage units, the onboard hybrid power systems remain prone to significant power quality degradation and instability under transient conditions such as wide-range load transients, partial source failures, and pulsed loads with a high peak-to-average ratio. To address these issues, this article proposes a composite controller that integrates a fixed-time sliding mode disturbance observer (FTSMDO) with a prescribed performance controller (PPC). The FTSMDO is designed to estimate lumped disturbances, upon which a PPC is developed using the backstepping method. This approach enhances the system’s capability for rapid voltage recovery under various transient events and ensures large-signal stability. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed composite control strategy is validated through both simulation and experimental results.

title: Novel Left-and-Right Two-Step Discrete ZNN Model for Time-Dependent Nonlinear Optimization With Equality and Inequality Constraints Applied to Robot Manipulator
authors: Min Yang, Zihan Zhu, Jian Li, Pengfei Guo, Hui Zhang, Shuai Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: For solving time-dependent nonlinear optimization with equality and inequality constraints (TDNO-EICs), a continuous-time zeroing neural network (CZNN) model is developed to achieve real-time adaptation to system dynamics. Inequality constraints are converted into an equivalent equality form through auxiliary variables, enabling a unified CZNN formulation. To obtain high-precision discrete ZNN (DZNN) solvers, this work completes the left-and-right two-step (LRTS) discretization framework by deriving the full second- and third-order LRTS time-discretization (TD) formulas and showing that no fourth-order LRTS rule exists due to incompatible order conditions. Based on these formulas, the 2-order and 3-order LRTS-DZNN algorithms are established. Rigorous convergence, stability, and sampling jitter robustness analyses verify their theoretical correctness and numerical experiments demonstrate the expected accuracy orders. Finally, simulation and physical tests on a UR5 robot manipulator confirm that the proposed LRTS-DZNN algorithms offer strong real-time tracking precision and practical effectiveness for dynamic optimization tasks.

title: SURE: A High-Performance, Efficient, and Secure Serverless Framework Based on Unikernels_supp1-3697819.pdf
authors: Shixiong Qi
publish: N/A
abstract: Current serverless platforms face substantial overhead from kernel-based networking and per-function sidecars. In addition, container-based runtimes suffer from excessive startup times and limited isolation. These limitations motivate the need for a more efficient and secure design. We present SURE, a unikernel-based serverless framework that combines rapid function startup with a high-performance, secure data plane. SURE enables distributed zero-copy communication through seamless integration of a userspace zero-copy TCP/IP stack (Z-stack) with intra-node shared memory processing. To eliminate the inefficiency of per-function sidecars, SURE introduces a lightweight library-based sidecar, which reduces CPU overhead by over two orders of magnitude compared to traditional userspace sidecars. For security, SURE leverages Intel's Memory Protection Keys (MPK) to enforce fine-grained, page-level isolation in the shared memory data plane and to isolate the Trusted Computing Base (TCB) components in the function runtime (e.g., library-based sidecar, scheduler, etc) from untrusted user code. This is complemented by memory-pool-based security domains that isolate each function chain, ensuring scalability to large deployments. SURE further integrates a combination of binary inspection, W <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\oplus$</tex-math></inline-formula> X enforcement, and TCB-page blacklisting to prevent MPK privilege escalation within the single-address-space unikernel. These combined efforts create a more secure and efficient data plane with improved performance. Our evaluation shows that SURE improves throughput by 6×-8× compared to SPRIGHT, a high-performance serverless platform.

title: Thermo-switchable Broadband Multifunctional Polarized Metamaterial
authors: Zheng Qin, Jian Liu, Zihan Xu, Shouwei Wang, Yongzhou Zhao, Weishuai Chen, Haihong Yu
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: Vanadium oxide-based metamaterials represent a promising candidate for developing integrated, low power, and high-performance photonic devices. However, infrared metamaterial devices remain constrained by simplistic functionalities, narrow bandwidths, and insufficient extinction ratios. In this letter, we propose a vanadium oxide-based metal–dielectric–metal metamaterial that simultaneously enables broadband linear-polarization filtering and thermally tunable polarization-selective absorption in the mid-infrared region. The device maintains a high average transmission extinction ratio of up to 911 over a broad spectral range. Meanwhile, under backward TM-polarized incidence, the absorptance can be thermally switched from 12.4% to 96.6% over 2.56–5.47 μm, with an average absorption extinction ratio of 51. These results demonstrate a compact and reconfigurable platform for infrared polarization manipulation.

title: Multilevel Control Strategy of Human-Exoskeleton Cooperative Motion via Gait Optimization and Fixed-time Adaptive Techniques
authors: Qing Guo, Haoran Zhan, Yuanchao Cao, Jiyu Zhang, Zongyu Zuo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: A multilevel control strategy is proposed in a lower-limb exoskeleton to reduce interaction torques and enhance compliance during human-exoskeleton cooperative motion. First, a gait dataset encompassing various movement patterns is constructed through gait acquisition experiments conducted on our lightweight device, with dynamic time warping (DTW) employed for data alignment. At the high level, dynamic movement primitives (DMPs) combined with Gaussian mixture models (GMM) and Gaussian mixture regression (GMR) are utilized to learn multiple demonstration trajectories and generate reference trajectories. At the middle level, an admittance controller is designed to derive the desired exoskeleton trajectory from human-exoskeleton interaction torques. At the low level, an adaptive fixed-time controller, incorporating barrier Lyapunov functions (BLF) and fuzzy logic systems (FLS), is developed to address model uncertainties and output constraints. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated through both simulations and experiments in active and passive training modes, demonstrating robust tracking, bounded error dynamics, and reduced interaction torques.

title: DNN Obfuscation with Contrastive Learning for Efficient Defense against Side-Channel Attacks_supp1-3697449.pdf
authors: Guiyuan Jiang
publish: N/A
abstract: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) have achieved no table advancements across various domains. Yet, they remain vulnerable to Side-Channel Attacks (SCAs) which expose them to model theft, posing substantial security threats. Existing countermeasures for mitigating SCAs using DNN obfuscation often involve impractical hardware modifications or suffer from poor scalability due to the need for time-consuming deployment on target platforms. We introduce CLOb-DNN (Contrastive Learning Obfuscation for DNNs), a novel contrastive learning based framework that improves both the efficiency and robust ness of DNN obfuscation against SCAs. By leveraging triplet networks, we effectively reduce the need for repeated physical deployments, substantially improving obfuscation efficiency while ensuring that the obfuscated DNNs meet latency constraints. Our framework integrates architecture-level and scheduling-level obfuscation to enhance stability and avoid costly modifications to backend source codes. We perform extensive evaluations using multiple state-of-the-art attack models to demonstrate the robustness of our approach against diverse threats. Experiments show that our method reduces obfuscation time by approximately 90%, achieves superior defense capability, and successfully meets various latency requirements, thereby offering a practical and effective solution for protecting DNN IPs against SCAs.

title: Particle Trapping and Optical Conveyor Belt Based on a Triple-Core Fiber
authors: Kai Zhang, Hongwei Li, Tingting Yuan, Youxing Li, Xiaotong Zhang, Libo Yuan
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: We propose and demonstrate an optical tweezer technique based on a triple-core fiber. The fiber tweezer supports two independent optical potential wells at its fiber tip, enabling the trapping and optical transport of two biological cells. The light field emitted from the central core of the fiber probe is axially focused via a high-refractive-index glass microsphere to form the first optical potential well. The two symmetric side cores of the fiber probe collectively form a second strongly converging optical potential well. Experiments demonstrate that these two optical potential wells can independently trap two yeast cells along the axial direction, and short-distance optical transport of a single yeast cell between the dual traps is realized by varying the core powers. Theoretical simulations further reveal that this transport function arises from the combined action of the two optical potential wells. The proposed scheme can serve as a fundamental building block for all-optical microfluidic chip technology.

title: Multi-Modal Position Estimation of Autonomous Vehicles using Anomaly Detection and Kalman Filter
authors: Chan-Ho Hwang, Jeon-Hyeok Lee, Tae-Hyoung Park
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Accurate localization is crucial for the safe navigation of autonomous vehicles, as it directly affects path planning and obstacle avoidance. However, Global Positioning System (GPS) and Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) sensors, commonly used for position estimation, have inherent limitations: GPS is prone to signal loss or multi path effects in urban environments, while LiDAR can be impacted by adverse weather or reflective surfaces. These issues often introduce anomalies in the position data, leading to unreliable localization results. To address these challenges, we propose a robust localization framework combining a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-Transformer Autoencoder for anomaly detection with a Multi Sensor Kalman Filter (MSKF). The anomaly detection model dynamically assesses the reliability of GPS and LiDAR data, allowing the MSKF to adjust the measurement covariance based on sensor conditions. Experimental results on the KITTI dataset and real-world highway scenarios demonstrate that our method improves position accuracy and robustness against sensor anomalies.

title: Anonymous and Privacy-Preserving Post-Quantum Key Encapsulation-Based Authentication Scheme for UAV-Assisted Internet of Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Mohd Shariq, Norziana Jamil, Gopal Singh Rawat, Ashok Kumar Das, Sisay Yigezu, Mehedi Masud, Amir Hamzah Abd Ghafar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The rapid adoption of uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAV) to assist the Internet of Autonomous Vehicles (IoAV) necessitates authentication mechanisms that ensure anonymity, privacy preservation, and quantum resistance while accommodating high mobility and constrained computational resources. However, existing lattice-based authentication schemes for vehicular networks often exhibit high computational complexity, lack anonymity, or are unsuitable for UAV-assisted architectures. To overcome these challenges, this paper introduces <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm{P}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>AS-IoAV, an anonymous and privacy-preserving post-quantum authentication scheme that integrates the CRYSTALS-Kyber key encapsulation mechanism with lightweight bitwise XOR operations to achieve enhanced efficiency. The proposed scheme offers mutual authentication, anonymity, and resistance to both classical and quantum attacks, as validated through formal real-or-random security analysis and automated verification using the Scyther tool. Comparative evaluations show that <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm{P}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>AS-IoAV significantly reduces the computational and communication overheads compared with existing post-quantum and lattice-based schemes, while improving security and scalability. The findings presented in this paper demonstrate that the proposed scheme delivers a practical, secure, and quantum-resistant authentication framework that is well-suited to next-generation UAV-assisted vehicular networks.
